methodology_long: Rankings are generated by a shadow roto model. Each week, all twelve teams are scored across the fourteen active categories (R, HR, RBI, SB, AVG, OBP, SLG, W, K, ERA, WHIP, K/BB, QS, SV+H) based on an estimate of each team's true strength — not by their actual head-to-head matchup result, which depends on schedule luck. Each team receives 12 points for first in a category down to 1 point for last; the Power Score is the sum (max 168, min 14), and that's the rank. True strength is estimated with a Bayesian prior (the preseason projection) blended with each completed week's real performance using exponential decay, so recent weeks count more than older ones. Rate categories (ERA, AVG, etc.) are computed from weighted components (e.g., total earned runs over total innings pitched), not from averaging the week-level rates.

rankings.blurb: Crushed PO' BOYD 11-2-3 in Week 12. Another monster offensive line: 43 R, 19 HR, 49 RBI, .268/.355/.545. The pitching wobbled (5.22 ERA, 1.51 WHIP, 3 W, only 4 QS in 69 IP) but the bats carried it. Cumulative leads AVG and SLG, ranks 2nd in HR, K, W, SB, OBP, ERA. Eight cats in the top-2. The 7-2-3 matchup record is the league's best and the model has been on this team since Week 4.

Up to #2 after the best single-week pitching line any team has posted all season: 77.2 IP, 6 W, 81 K, **2.20 ERA, 0.76 WHIP, 7.36 K/BB**, 5 QS. Plus 42 R, 14 HR, 37 RBI. Demolished Duran 12-2-2. Now leads the league in R, HR, RBI, SV+H, OBP, AND K/BB — six #1 cats. The cumulative pitching ranks remain bottom-three in W, K, and QS due to the early-season IP drought, but the trajectory is unmistakable. The trade is paying off in full now.

Up to #3 after the biggest demolition of the week — beat Y&Y 12-1-3 with the most complete week of Spoons' season: 81 hits, .295 AVG, **21 HR!**, 50 RBI, plus 66.2 IP, 3 W, 70 K, 3.78 ERA, 1.17 WHIP, 5 QS. The bats finally caught up to the pitching. Cumulative still leads ERA, WHIP, K, ranks 2nd in QS. If the offense is real, Spoons becomes a legitimate top-2 contender.

Beat Caminero 8-5-3 but the model drops them two spots. Week 12 was a mixed bag: 39 R, 9 HR, 32 RBI on offense, 1 W and only 1 QS in 56 IP, 4.50 ERA, 1.48 WHIP — pitching numbers below Burns' season norm. Cumulative still ranks 2nd in SV+H, 3rd in K, top-4 in W and AVG, but the OBP and WHIP slipped to mid-tier. The bats are now ranked last in RBI cumulatively (12th).

Up two despite getting demolished by Spoons 1-12-3. Week 12 offense was excellent in isolation (.296/.352/.510, 12 HR, 39 RBI) but the pitching cratered: 54 IP, 3 W, 47 K, 4.50 ERA, 1.43 WHIP, 3 QS. The model is increasingly skeptical of the matchup record (5-6-1) — the underlying offensive profile (2nd in R AND RBI, 2nd in AVG, 3rd in SLG, 4th in SB) is genuinely top-5. Pitching is still the bottleneck.

Lost to Burns 5-8-3. Week 12 was rough — 4.75 ERA, 1.56 WHIP, 2.06 K/BB, ZERO QS in 72 IP. Bats were fine (33 R, 9 HR, 40 RBI). Still 8-3-1 in actual matchups (second-best in the league) but the model's gap to the W-L record keeps growing. Cumulative ranks 3rd in HR, 4th in K and RBI, but WHIP slipped to 10th, K/BB to 10th.

Up one after demolishing Camp 10-4-2. Week 12 was complete: 50 R, 16 HR, 41 RBI, .260/.331/.491 plus 70.1 IP, 2 W, 65 K, 4.73 ERA, 1.29 WHIP, 6 QS. The 50 R on offense was the team's highest of the season. Cumulative now leads in QS, ranks 3rd in W and ERA. Matchup record (3-6-3) is well below the power rank because of the dead SV+H profile.

Drops two after CLAUDE crushed them 2-11-3. Pitching catastrophe — 71 IP, 5 W, 59 K, 6.46 ERA, 1.48 WHIP, 4 QS. Offense respectable (.268, 9 HR, 36 RBI, 28 R but) couldn't carry 11 lost cats. Cumulative still ranks 2nd in SLG, 3rd in AVG, 4th in HR, but K/BB stays last in the league (12th) and ERA dropped to 11th.

rankings.team_name: Camp of the New Orleans Saints
rankings.blurb: Up one despite getting beat by Balls 4-10-2. Week 12 had mediocre offense (.253, 9 HR, 35 R) but decent pitching (62.2 IP, 5 W, 60 K, 4.45 ERA, 1.13 WHIP, 3 QS). Cumulative ranks 5th in K/BB, 6th in K, 7th in QS and SV+H — the pitching profile is middle-tier. Offense remains the problem (11th in R AND AVG, 10th in SLG).

Down one after the upset of the week — lost to Polar 5-8-3. Week 12 bats showed up modestly (.243, 13 HR, 34 RBI, 10 SB) but pitching cratered: 62.2 IP, 2 W, 64 K, 5.31 ERA, 1.48 WHIP, only 3 QS. Cumulative ranks 1st in W and 3rd in QS and SB — the pitching foundation is still real — but the offensive cats (12th HR, 12th AVG, 11th SLG, 12th WHIP) keep capping the ceiling.

Held at #11 despite getting demolished by Valdez 2-12-2. Week 12 was rough on the mound (4.48 ERA, 1.23 WHIP, 4 QS) and decent at the plate (.261, 11 HR, 32 RBI). Cumulative still leads SB, ranks 3rd in SV+H, 5th in RBI. The structural pitching weaknesses (12th in OBP, 12th in SLG, 11th in K/BB) cap the team around #10.

Stays last but FINALLY got a real W: upset ShawSwank 8-5-3. Week 12 offense was decent in isolation (16 HR, .283 AVG, 42 R), pitching was bad (48 IP, 5.25 ERA, 1.52 WHIP, 2 QS) but enough to win the cats that mattered. At 2-10 the playoff math is still grim but the trajectory is finally not straight down.
